title: Newborn Prophylaxis: Parental Informed Consent and Refusal
subtitle: Healthy newborn parents decline the IM vitamin K injection and question the eye ointment and hepatitis B vaccine. The nurse educates without coercion, respects informed parental decisions, documents, and knows when a refusal must be escalated.

patient.name: Baby Girl Larsen
patient.admitDate: Term newborn, vigorous, feeding well; mother HBsAg-negative. Parents decline the routine IM vitamin K injection and have questions about erythromycin eye ointment and the hepatitis B birth dose. Nurse providing education and informed-consent counseling.

vitals.temp: Educated on VKDB risk; considering oral vitamin K

labs.r: Hep B birth dose not time-critical.
HBIG + vaccine ≤12 h if mother HBsAg+.

nursesNote: Baby Girl Larsen, term newborn, vigorous and feeding; mother HBsAg-negative. Parents decline the IM vitamin K injection (asking about oral vitamin K) and have questions about eye ointment and hepatitis B. Parents are the newborn's surrogate decision-makers; informed consent applies. The nurse gives accurate, non-coercive education (benefits/risks/alternatives), uses teach-back, respects an informed decision for non-emergent prophylaxis, documents objectively, and notifies the pediatrician. Refusal of clearly life-saving emergency care — or care whose absence causes serious imminent harm (medical neglect) — exceeds parental authority and is escalated (provider/ethics/social work/CPS).
providerOrders: Educate parents on newborn prophylaxis: vitamin K, eye ointment, hepatitis B
Obtain informed consent; document acceptance or informed refusal
If mother is HBsAg-positive: hepatitis B vaccine + HBIG within 12 hours
Notify the pediatrician of any refusal; arrange follow-up
Do not coerce or administer declined interventions covertly

opts.text: Parents are the surrogate decision-makers for their newborn's care
Informed consent for the newborn includes benefits, risks, and alternatives
Vitamin K prevents vitamin K deficiency bleeding, including serious late bleeding
Erythromycin eye ointment prevents newborn eye infection (ophthalmia neonatorum)
Parents can be forced to accept any recommended newborn intervention
A hepatitis B birth dose is routine; if the mother is HBsAg-positive, add HBIG within 12 hours
The nurse provides non-coercive, accurate education and uses teach-back
A fully informed parental refusal of a non-emergent intervention is respected
The refusal and the education provided must be documented objectively
Covertly giving a declined medication violates the parents' rights
Parental authority allows refusing clearly life-saving emergency care
If a refusal poses serious, imminent, preventable harm, escalate (provider/ethics/CPS)

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Order the response to the refusal

opts.text: Provide thorough education on benefits, risks, and alternatives
Confirm understanding with teach-back and answer questions
Respect an informed decision for a non-emergent intervention
Notify the provider/pediatrician and document objectively
Arrange follow-up; escalate only if the refusal poses serious imminent harm
Provide clear education on benefits, risks, and alternatives
Use teach-back to confirm the parents understand
Respect a fully informed refusal of a non-emergent intervention
Document the education, the decision, and provider notification
Notify the provider/pediatrician of the refusal
Escalate (provider/ethics/social work) if refusal poses serious imminent harm
Shame or pressure the parents into consenting
Give the vitamin K injection covertly after they declined
Chart "parents refused" with no education or notification

steps.title: Take Action
steps.inst: Rank the order of actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Educate the parents calmly and without coercion
Use teach-back to ensure they understand the risks of declining
Honor a fully informed, non-emergent decision
Document the teaching, decision, and notification
Arrange follow-up and reassess if circumstances change
The nurse educates the parents and they make an informed choice
The refusal is documented with the education given and provider notified
The parents accept the eye ointment and hepatitis B after teaching
For an HBsAg-positive mother, HBIG and hep B vaccine are given within 12 hours
The nurse injects vitamin K secretly after the parents declined
The nurse calls the parents "bad parents" to pressure them
The refusal is charted with no teaching, alternatives, or notification
A clearly life-saving emergency treatment is withheld because a parent objects

reference: Aligned to the NCSBN NCLEX Test Plan client-need category: Psychosocial Integrity, using the NCSBN Clinical Judgment Measurement Model. Written from standard nursing references. Pending faculty review.
